conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 6 years old and I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, **are you responsible for Alex?** This means do you live with him and are you responsible for paying for things like his food, clothing, and other needs?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. Alex lives with me and I'm responsible for all his needs - his food, clothes, school things, everything really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's great, thank you. Now I need to ask: **Are you or Alex currently living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, we both live in the UK. We're here in England.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** For example, Alex's other parent or another family member?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. One final question: **Do you have immigration permission or the right to live in the UK?** This could be through British citizenship, settled status, or other legal permission to reside here.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have the right to live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided, you meet all the necessary requirements:

✓ You are responsible for Alex - you live with him and cover his needs
✓ You both live in the UK
✓ Alex is 6 years old (under the age limit of 16, or 20 if in approved education/training)
✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
✓ You have the right to live in the UK

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit for Alex. Please note that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay a High Income Child Benefit Charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. The claimant meets all the key eligibility criteria: (1) They are responsible for Alex - they live with him and pay for his needs including food, clothing, and school things; (2) Both the claimant and Alex live in the UK (England); (3) Alex is 6 years old, which is under 16 (or under 20 if in approved education or training); (4) No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (5) The claimant has the right to live in the UK. All conditions for Child Benefit eligibility are satisfied.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for their 6-year-old son Alex. They meet all the necessary requirements: they are responsible for Alex, both live in the UK, the child is under 16, no one else is claiming for him, and the claimant has the right to live in the UK.
